creator: hemi@puresimplicity.net
text:
On Sat, Jun 09, 2007 at 09:25:02AM +0400, Sergey Matveychuk wrote:
> % pkg_version -t 2.3_1 1.75_2,1
> <
> 
> portupgrade checks version even with -o switch. This is a feature by 
> design. I'll place it in my TODO list but I'm not sure I can fix it ASAP.

Thanks for the reply.

Shouldn't -o override that behavior, though? Considering -o's general use (for
me, at least) is replacing closely-related but often different-version
packages and takes action by the admin to use (specifying the new origin),
shouldn't the version number be disregarded when using -o?

I'm guessing the version number comparison is an ASCII string comparison
instead of something that actually considers them as numbers?

Thanks,

Josh
-- 
Josh Tolbert
hemi@puresimplicity.net  ||  http://www.puresimplicity.net/~hemi/

Security is mostly a superstition. It does not exist in nature, nor
do the children of men as a whole experience it. Avoiding danger
is no safer in the long run than outright exposure. Life is either
a daring adventure, or nothing.
    -- Helen Keller
